name: Canadian Yellow Split-Pea Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 1/2 oz Sorted uncooked yellow split peas
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the split peas.
2. In a 4-quart saucepan, combine the split peas, water, and salt. Bring to a boil; remove from heat and let soak for 1 hour.
3. Heat oil in a 10-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add bacon and cook, stirring frequently, until browned.
4. Stir in onions and carrots and cook, stirring occasionally, until onions are translucent.
5. Add the cooked bacon, onions, and carrots to the split pea mixture and stir to combine.
6. Add the remaining ingredients (sage leaves, ground allspice, and salt) except parsley, and bring to a boil.
7. Reduce heat to low, cover, and let simmer, stirring occasionally to prevent the mixture from sticking to the bottom of the pan, until peas are softened and the mixture thickens, about 45 minutes.
8. If the soup becomes too thick, add up to an additional cup of water.
9. Serve each portion sprinkled with fresh parsley.
Makes 4 servings.
